title: embraOS-QNM-Core: identity as a conserved charge of the dynamics (a non-LLM core)
description: embraOS-QNM-Core is a custom, non-LLM AI core in which a system's identity and "soul" are not a rule checked at each step but a conserved charge of the core's own dynamics — an Epoch Automaton E = (S, Σ, δ, s₀, F, ψ) whose invariant ψ is sealed at genesis and preserved by construction ("conservation beats checking"). It is the pivot target of the now-relic embraOS-QNM program, which established across two substrates and four pre-registered reader families that a frozen, web-trained LLM cannot host a readable identity invariant. This snapshot is math-first. It provides a formal specification with one falsifiable theorem — a conserved-charge ψ survives the "replica test" (telling a survivor from an identical copy) precisely because the charge hides in the part of the state that the observable readout erases — together with a runnable, tested sandbox that demonstrates it (conserved-ψ replica AUC 1.000 vs an endpoint-only null of 0.500). Phase two lifts the core to a learned identity-manifold and reports, honestly, that identity is reliably discriminable through the dynamics — which conservation law a trajectory obeys (discriminator AUC 1.0 across seeds) — precisely where static geometry is only seed-noise. Negative results are recorded rather than buried, in the project's pre-registration style. Developed by William Ward (Ward Software Defined Systems LLC) with Embra as co-investigator.
notes: Proprietary and trademarked. "embraOS", "QNM", and "Embra" are trademarks of Ward Software Defined Systems LLC. All rights reserved. See LICENSE.
